title: DependencyUpdaterConfig
description: Input contract for bin/gen-dependency-updater.sh — describes what kind of dependency-updater config to emit (Dependabot vs. Renovate), which ecosystems to enable, and basic scheduling. The schema is small because both Dependabot and Renovate already have their own deep config grammars; nyann's role is to pick sensible defaults and surface a few high-impact knobs, not to re-implement either system's full surface.

updater.description: Which dependency-updater to generate config for. `dependabot` emits a per-ecosystem YAML at .github/dependabot.yml. `renovate` emits a single JSON at renovate.json that uses `config:recommended` so Renovate auto-detects ecosystems — the `ecosystems` field is then only used to validate that at least one is present (Renovate without any ecosystem in the repo would never produce PRs).

ecosystems.description: Per-ecosystem entries to emit. Order is preserved in the output so a stable diff survives re-generation. Each ecosystem maps to one Dependabot `package-ecosystem` block; Renovate consumes only the list (presence-check) since `config:recommended` handles per-ecosystem behavior.

ecosystem.description: Dependabot's `package-ecosystem` value. `npm` covers npm/pnpm/yarn/bun (Dependabot routes through the npm ecosystem regardless of which lockfile is present). For monorepos, declare one entry per workspace path (caller-side responsibility — this script doesn't auto-walk).

directory.description: Repo-relative path of the manifest. `/` for the repo root; subpaths like `/packages/api` for workspaces. Must start with `/` per Dependabot's convention.

schedule_interval.description: How often Dependabot polls this ecosystem. Default `weekly` balances PR-noise vs. responsiveness; bump to `daily` for security-critical projects.

open_pull_requests_limit.description: Max open Dependabot PRs for this ecosystem at any time. Five is enough headroom for routine updates without overwhelming review queues; raise for high-velocity repos.

labels.description: GitHub labels applied to opened PRs. `automated` is recommended so a CI label-filter can opt these out of expensive-job matrices when desired.

grouping.description: Bundle multiple updates into a single PR. `off` = one PR per update (Dependabot default). `minor-patch` = group minor + patch updates per ecosystem, keep majors separate (recommended). `all` = group everything regardless of semver tier (use only when review bandwidth is the bottleneck).

snapshot_version.description: Optional. Records the nyann release whose defaults the emitted config snapshots. Surfaced as a comment in the generated YAML/JSON so a future operator can tell when the file was last regenerated. Free-form; not enforced.
